az.description: The root command for the Azure CLI, a cross-platform command-line tool for creating and managing Azure resources. All Azure CLI commands begin with 'az' followed by a command group, optional subgroup, a command, and parameters. Supports JSON, table, TSV, and YAML output formats.

login.signature: az login [--use-device-code] [--tenant TENANT_ID] [--service-principal -u CLIENT_ID -p SECRET --tenant TENANT_ID]
login.description: Authenticates to Azure, opening a browser for interactive login or using device code, service principal, or managed identity credentials.

account set.description: Sets the active subscription for subsequent CLI commands when the account has access to multiple subscriptions.

configure.description: Manages Azure CLI configuration including default resource group, region, and output format preferences.

az group.description: Manages Azure Resource Groups, which are logical containers that hold related Azure resources for an application. Resource groups allow you to manage, monitor, and control access to resources as a single unit and define the region where metadata is stored.

create.signature: az group create --name NAME --location LOCATION [--tags KEY=VALUE]
create.description: Creates a new resource group in the specified Azure region with optional tags for organization and billing.

delete.description: Deletes a resource group and all resources contained within it. This operation is irreversible.

list.description: Lists all resource groups in the active subscription with optional JMESPath query filtering.

az vm.description: Manages Azure Virtual Machines, including creation, configuration, starting, stopping, resizing, and deletion. Supports Linux and Windows VMs with various sizes, images, networking options, and disk configurations.

create.signature: az vm create --resource-group RG --name NAME --image IMAGE [--size SIZE] [--admin-username USER] [--generate-ssh-keys]
create.description: Creates a new virtual machine with the specified image, size, and authentication configuration in the given resource group.

start.description: Powers on a stopped or deallocated virtual machine, resuming billing for compute resources.

stop.description: Powers off a virtual machine but keeps it allocated, still incurring compute charges until deallocated.

deallocate.description: Deallocates a virtual machine, releasing compute resources and stopping billing while retaining disks and network configuration.

az storage.description: Manages Azure Storage resources including storage accounts, blob containers, file shares, queues, and tables. Provides commands for creating accounts, uploading and downloading data, managing access policies, and configuring storage features.

account create.signature: az storage account create --name NAME --resource-group RG --location LOCATION --sku {Standard_LRS,Standard_GRS}
account create.description: Creates a new storage account with the specified redundancy level, access tier, and networking configuration.

blob upload.signature: az storage blob upload --account-name ACCOUNT --container-name CONTAINER --file FILE --name BLOB_NAME
blob upload.description: Uploads a local file as a blob to the specified container in Azure Blob Storage.

blob download.signature: az storage blob download --account-name ACCOUNT --container-name CONTAINER --name BLOB_NAME --file DEST
blob download.description: Downloads a blob from Azure Blob Storage to a local file path.

az webapp.description: Manages Azure App Service web applications, which host web apps, REST APIs, and mobile backends. Supports deployment from source code, containers, or zip packages with built-in scaling, SSL, authentication, and continuous deployment capabilities.

create.signature: az webapp create --resource-group RG --plan PLAN --name NAME [--runtime RUNTIME]
create.description: Creates a new web app in an App Service plan with the specified runtime stack (e.g., NODE:18-lts, PYTHON:3.11).

deploy.signature: az webapp deploy --resource-group RG --name NAME --src-path PATH [--type {zip,war,jar,ear,startup}]
deploy.description: Deploys code or a package to the web app using the Kudu deployment engine.

log tail.signature: az webapp log tail --resource-group RG --name NAME
log tail.description: Streams live log output from the web app to the terminal for real-time debugging.

az aks.description: Manages Azure Kubernetes Service (AKS) clusters for running containerized applications. Provides commands for cluster lifecycle management, node pool scaling, version upgrades, and kubectl credential retrieval.

create.signature: az aks create --resource-group RG --name NAME --node-count N [--node-vm-size SIZE] [--generate-ssh-keys]
create.description: Creates a new managed Kubernetes cluster with the specified node count, VM size, and networking configuration.

get-credentials.description: Downloads and merges the cluster kubeconfig into ~/.kube/config for kubectl access.

az network.description: Manages Azure networking resources including virtual networks, subnets, network security groups, public IP addresses, load balancers, and DNS zones for building secure and scalable network architectures.

vnet create.signature: az network vnet create --resource-group RG --name NAME --address-prefix CIDR [--subnet-name NAME --subnet-prefix CIDR]
vnet create.description: Creates a virtual network with the specified address space and optional initial subnet.

nsg create.signature: az network nsg create --resource-group RG --name NAME
nsg create.description: Creates a network security group that contains access control rules for filtering network traffic.

az acr.description: Manages Azure Container Registry instances for storing and managing Docker container images and OCI artifacts. Supports image building, geo-replication, vulnerability scanning, and integration with AKS and App Service.

create.description: Creates a new container registry with the specified SKU tier determining storage, throughput, and features.

login.description: Authenticates the local Docker client with the Azure Container Registry for push and pull operations.

build.description: Builds a Docker image in the cloud using ACR Tasks without requiring a local Docker daemon.

String.description: Text data type representing a sequence of characters.

Number.description: Numeric data type for integer and floating-point values.

Boolean.description: Logical type with two values: true and false.

Array.description: Ordered collection of elements accessible by index.

Object.description: Collection of key-value pairs (properties and methods).

null.description: Represents the intentional absence of any value.

undefined.description: Represents an uninitialized or missing value.

print.description: Output a value to the standard output stream.

length.description: Returns the number of elements in a collection or characters in a string.
